message:
fix(test): Fail fast when test failure tracking fails - CRITICAL for CI

Fixed 4 silent failures in test failure recording that could cause CI to
report incorrect results:

Before: If memory allocation failed when recording test failures:
- Failed test names were silently dropped
- Failure records were silently skipped
- CI summary would be incomplete/wrong
- Test failures could go unreported

Changes in test_commands.zig:
- Line 813: name_copy allocation now exits on failure (was: continue)
- Line 817: compile_fail record now exits on failure (was: catch {})
- Line 835: timeout name allocation now exits on failure (was: continue)
- Line 839: timeout record now exits on failure (was: catch {})
- Line 851: failed name allocation now exits on failure (was: continue)
- Line 855: failed record now exits on failure (was: catch {})

All failures now:
- Print CRITICAL error message explaining the issue
- Use std.process.exit(1) to ensure CI catches the problem
- Never silently drop test failure information

This is CRITICAL for CI reliability - we MUST know when tests fail.
'Fail fast fail loud' - if we can't track failures, we must abort.
